lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: canvas IS mounted (PaneType::AtelierEditor, default Atelier tab) but host consumes ONLY ResizePlacement/AssignSection; PlaceBlock/AddCard/Group/RemovePlacement/SemanticEdge/VisualEdgeAdded/ViewportChanged fall to catch-all None (in-code comment claiming MT-026 paths own them is FALSE) - drop-to-place, +Text card, remove, edge drawing, viewport persistence are inert. No initial getCanvasBoard fetch at mount; post-mutation fetch_board writes into an immediately-dropped cell (pane doc comment claiming refresh feeds back is FALSE). Mounted canvas renders permanently empty. Fix: wire remaining 7 event kinds to existing CanvasBoardClient + initial fetch + board-cell drain into the widget; correct false doc comments.

scope.summary:
Goal: Port the React LoomCanvasBoard (MT-261 parity) to the native egui frontend (crate handshake-native, lib path handshake_native::). The canvas board is a spatial canvas where LoomBlocks are placed as reference cards (not copies) with real pan/zoom, drag-to-place, edge drawing (semantic and visual), grouping, and viewport persistence. The board itself is a typed LoomBlock (content_type=canvas); placements and edges are authoritative PostgreSQL/EventLedger state. Expected diff shape: one new file src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/canvas_board.rs containing LoomCanvasBoard widget, edits to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs.

Exact files to create or edit:
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/canvas_board.rs — LoomCanvasBoard widget
- EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs — pub mod canvas_board; + re-export

Reuse (do not reinvent): the spatial-canvas painting/pan/zoom primitives in the existing WP-011 module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs (generic board surface), the Loom graph view in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs (block/edge model + graph rendering), the shared HTTP client in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (all backend calls route through it), command/selection/clipboard wiring via src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs, AccessKit exposure via src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/* (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs), and MCP tool/action exposure via src/frontend/handshake_native/src/mcp/* where the canvas surface must be model-drivable.

Exact APIs / React reference:
- PRIMARY REACT REFERENCE: app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.tsx — the complete React implementation. Key behaviors to port exactly:
  (a) placeBlockOnCanvas(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, {placedBlockId, x, y, w, h}) on drop (api.ts ~1617+)
  (b) createCanvasCard(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, {title, body, x, y, w, h}) for free-text cards
  (c) updateCanvasBoardViewport(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, {pan_x, pan_y, zoom}) on viewport change
  (d) updateCanvasPlacement(workspaceId, placementId, {groupId}) for grouping
  (e) removeCanvasPlacement(workspaceId, placementId) for removal (source block kept)
  (f) createLoomEdge(workspaceId, {sourceBlockId, targetBlockId, edgeType:'mention'}) for semantic edges
  (g) addCanvasVisualEdge(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, {fromPlacementId, toPlacementId}) for visual edges
  (h) getCanvasBoard(workspaceId, canvasBlockId) -> LoomCanvasBoardView {board, placements, visual_edges}
  (i) getLoomBlock(workspaceId, blockId) to resolve live block previews (reference, not copy)
- Backend bindings: all functions from app/src/lib/api.ts in the section starting at line 1617 (LOOM_CANVAS_BOARD_SCHEMA_ID block), called through src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Define CanvasPlacementCard { placement_id: String, placed_block_id: String, x: f32, y: f32, w: f32, h: f32, z_index: i32, group_id: Option<String>, live_title: Option<String>, live_content_type: Option<String>, selected: bool }.
2. Define VisualEdge { visual_edge_id: String, from_placement_id: String, to_placement_id: String }.
3. Define LoomCanvasBoard widget state: { workspace_id, canvas_block_id, placements: Vec<CanvasPlacementCard>, visual_edges: Vec<VisualEdge>, pan: Vec2, zoom: f32, selected: HashSet<String>, edge_mode: EdgeMode (Semantic | Visual), edge_from: Option<String>, status: String, loading: bool, error: Option<String> }.
4. On init: call getCanvasBoard -> populate placements + visual_edges + pan + zoom. Then for each placement, call getLoomBlock to resolve live_title + live_content_type. Store results in placements (ref semantics: a missing block shows '(stale reference)', never a copy).
5. Render loop in show():
   a. Top toolbar strip (using egui::TopBottomPanel or manual strip at top of allocated rect):
      - Pan-left / Pan-right buttons (author_id='canvas.pan-left'/'canvas.pan-right'): adjust pan.x by ±40; call updateCanvasBoardViewport on release.
      - Zoom-out / Zoom-in buttons (author_id='canvas.zoom-out'/'canvas.zoom-in'): adjust zoom by ±0.25 clamped [0.25, 4.0]; call updateCanvasBoardViewport.
      - Zoom value label (author_id='canvas.zoom-value').
      - '+ Text card' button (author_id='canvas.add-card'): calls createCanvasCard with default position (40, 40) and title=timestamp.
      - 'Group (N)' button (author_id='canvas.group'): enabled only if selected.len() >= 2; calls updateCanvasPlacement for each selected with a new group_id.
      - Edge mode dropdown/toggle (author_id='canvas.edge-mode'): Semantic | Visual.
      - 'Draw edge from selected' button (author_id='canvas.start-edge'): enabled if selected.len()==1; on click sets edge_from = selected[0].
   b. Status bar: single line of status text (author_id='canvas.status').
   c. Canvas surface (fills remaining rect):
      - Background: draw a dotted grid pattern using Painter::circle_filled at 24px spacing with radius 1px, color gray(203).
      - Apply pan+zoom transform: translate by pan, then scale by zoom (use Painter with transform via egui::emath::RectTransform or manual coordinate math).
      - Draw visual edges: for each VisualEdge, find from/to placements, draw dashed line between card centers (stroke 2px, Color32::from_gray(148), dash pattern 6/4).
      - Draw placement cards: for each CanvasPlacementCard, draw a rounded rect at (x,y,w,h) * zoom + pan. Border: if selected 2px blue, else 1px gray(203). White fill. Show live_title (bold 13px) and live_content_type (muted 11px). Show a remove button ('x') at top-right of card.
      - Click on empty area: deselect all + start pan drag.
      - Drag on empty area: pan (mouse delta added to pan; persist on release via updateCanvasBoardViewport).
      - Scroll wheel: zoom (multiply zoom by 1.15^delta; clamp; zoom toward pointer; persist).
      - Click on card: toggle selection (shift=additive); if edge_from is set and this card != edge_from, call drawEdge(edge_from, this placement_id); clear edge_from.
      - Drag FROM external source (egui DragAndDrop or OS drop): accept MIME 'application/x-handshake-loom-block' JSON {blockId, title}; compute drop position in canvas space = (screen_pos - pan) / zoom; call placeBlockOnCanvas; refresh.
6. Edge drawing: if edge_mode=Semantic, call createLoomEdge(source_block_id, target_block_id, 'mention'); if edge_mode=Visual, call addCanvasVisualEdge(from_placement_id, to_placement_id). Always refresh after.
7. AccessKit: each placement card gets author_id='canvas.placement.{placement_id}' role=Group, label=live_title, actions=[DefaultAction=select, Remove]. Remove button 'canvas.placement.{placement_id}.remove'. Toolbar buttons as listed above. Status bar 'canvas.status' role=Status. Register all nodes through src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/*.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: MT-021 must be complete for shared HTTP client (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs). No dependency on MT-022 through MT-025.

Risk if missed: Canvas board (Obsidian Canvas parity, MT-261) is absent from native frontend. This is a key differentiator surface.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Opening a canvas block loads the board from real PG (with >=2 placements seeded). Placements render as cards showing live block titles (reference, not copy text). The zoom value label shows '1.00x'.
AC2: Pan buttons adjust pan by 40px and the placements visibly shift. Viewport is persisted to backend via updateCanvasBoardViewport.
AC3: Zoom-in button increases zoom; zoom-out decreases it; zoom value label updates. Both clamp at [0.25, 4.0].

AC4: Dropping a Loom block (via drag with MIME 'application/x-handshake-loom-block') onto the canvas calls placeBlockOnCanvas and the new placement appears in the next refresh. The new card shows the live block title (proves reference not copy).
AC5: Clicking '+ Text card' calls createCanvasCard, a new placement appears, and its title reflects the created block's title (timestamp-based from React reference).

AC6: Selecting 2 cards (shift-click) and clicking 'Group (2)' calls updateCanvasPlacement for each with a shared group_id. The group_id attribute is visible in the AccessKit data-group-id of each affected card.

AC7: Drawing a Semantic edge (edge_mode=Semantic): click 'Draw edge from selected', click a second card, assert createLoomEdge API called with source and target block_ids. Drawing a Visual edge: same flow with edge_mode=Visual, assert addCanvasVisualEdge API called.
AC8: Clicking the remove 'x' button on a card calls removeCanvasPlacement and the card disappears from the board. Source block is NOT deleted (verify getLoomBlock still returns it).
AC9: AccessKit tree includes 'canvas.pan-left', 'canvas.pan-right', 'canvas.zoom-in', 'canvas.zoom-out', 'canvas.add-card', 'canvas.edge-mode', 'canvas.placement.{placement_id}' for each card.
AC10: Board with zero placements shows empty canvas with no panic and no 'stale reference' text.
proof_targets: PROOF1: cargo test -p handshake-native loom::canvas_board -- --nocapture passes unit tests for canvas-space coordinate transform ((screen-pan)/zoom) round-trip accuracy (<1px error).
PROOF2: kittest test with 2 seeded placements in real PG: assert 2 'canvas.placement.*' AccessKit nodes and their labels match the live block titles.

PROOF3: kittest drop-to-place test: simulate drop event at canvas position (100,100) with block_id='block-drop-1', assert placeBlockOnCanvas API call fired with correct x/y, assert 3 placement AccessKit nodes after refresh.
PROOF4: kittest edge-semantic test: select 'canvas.placement.p-001', click 'canvas.start-edge', click 'canvas.placement.p-002', assert createLoomEdge API call with correct source/target block_ids.

PROOF5: kittest remove test: click 'canvas.placement.p-001.remove', assert removeCanvasPlacement called and 'canvas.placement.p-001' absent from AccessKit tree; assert getLoomBlock('block-001') still returns 200.
PROOF6: Screenshot from kittest shows a non-white canvas with at least one card shape visible (rect with rounded corners).
implementation_notes: Canvas coordinate transform: canvas_pos = (screen_pos - pan) / zoom. Apply this to convert mouse position to canvas space for drop placement and card hit testing. Reuse the pan/zoom transform helpers already present in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs rather than reimplementing them.
Card hit testing: check if canvas_pos is within placement rect [x, y, x+w, y+h]. Iterate placements in reverse z_index order for correct hit priority.
Pan drag: detect pointer press on empty canvas (no card hit), track pointer delta each frame while button held, add to pan. On release, call updateCanvasBoardViewport.
Zoom to pointer: new_pan = pointer - (pointer - pan) * (new_zoom / old_zoom). This keeps the canvas point under the pointer stationary.
Grid drawing: iterate x from 0 to canvas_width step 24, y from 0 to canvas_height step 24; painter.circle_filled(canvas_to_screen(x,y), 1.0, gray(203)).

Dashed line for visual edges: egui::Painter does not have a native dash API. Implement by splitting the line segment into dash/gap segments manually: for a line from A to B, compute unit vector, alternate drawing solid segments of length 6 and skipping segments of length 4.

MIME drag: egui does not natively handle OS file drops but egui_winit exposes dropped files. For inter-panel drag, use egui::DragAndDrop payload with a custom type wrapping the block_id. Fall back to external OS drop via egui::ViewportCommand if available.
Avoid re-fetching live blocks on every frame: fetch once on load and on explicit refresh. Do not store live block content — only title and content_type (reference-only display).

Refresh after every mutation: all mutating API calls (place, create, remove, edge, group, viewport) are followed by a getCanvasBoard + getLoomBlock re-resolve cycle. Route every backend call through src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT builds on WP-KERNEL-011 deliverables. It reuses the existing modules src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs (generic spatial canvas), src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs (Loom block/edge model + graph render), src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (shared HTTP client), src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs (selection/clipboard/command bus), src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/* (AccessKit), and src/frontend/handshake_native/src/mcp/* (model-drivable surface). Those WP-011 modules must exist before this MT executes.

MODULE DIR + NAME (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): place the Loom canvas widget in the EXISTING graph/ dir (graph/canvas_board.rs - where MT-021..025 E3 widgets live), registered in graph/mod.rs - NOT a new loom/ dir. NOTE the NAME COLLISION: there is ALREADY a WP-011 ROOT module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs (the GENERIC spatial-canvas pan/zoom primitives to REUSE). The new graph::canvas_board is namespaced under graph:: and is DISTINCT from the root crate::canvas_board - reuse the root one's pan/zoom helpers (canvas_to_screen/screen_to_canvas, MC-1) rather than reimplementing. allowed_paths corrected to graph/ + backend_client.rs + tests/test_canvas_board.rs (contract under-scoped, as MT-021..025).

VERIFY THE CANVAS API + content_type AGAINST THE REAL BACKEND (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - MANDATORY, the MT-022/023/024 lesson): MT-022 found content_type='folder' wrong (loom_folders), MT-023 found 'tag_hub' wrong (MT-182 tags), MT-024 found 3 route assumptions wrong. So VERIFY read-only against src/backend/handshake_core/src/{api,storage}/loom.rs + app/src/lib/api.ts that EACH exists as assumed: content_type='canvas' (LoomBlockContentType variant?); the LoomCanvasBoardView{board,placements,visual_edges} shape + placement fields (placement_id/placed_block_id/x/y/w/h/z_index/group_id); and EVERY route (GET canvas, POST placements, POST cards, PUT viewport, PATCH placement groupId, DELETE placement, POST edges, POST visual-edges, GET block). Bind only VERIFIED shapes; record any correction. If canvas is a dedicated subsystem (like loom_folders/MT-182 tags), bind THAT.

BACKEND PROOF REALITY (MT-008/021-025 pattern): AC1-AC8 + PROOF2-5 need a LIVE Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with a seeded canvas block + >=2 placements + a taggable/droppable block. Bind the REAL endpoints; gate the live-data ACs behind the integration feature + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if unseeded - do NOT fake. The canvas_to_screen/screen_to_canvas round-trip (PROOF1 <1px, RISK-1/MC-1 - ONE pair used by both draw + hit-test), card hit-test, dashed-edge segmentation (RISK-5 short-edge solid fallback), zoom-to-pointer math, and the empty-board no-panic (AC10) are unit/kittest-testable STANDALONE with mock placements.

SEMANTICS + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): REFERENCE-NOT-COPY - placements show the LIVE block title via getLoomBlock fetched ONCE on load + on explicit refresh (NOT every frame; store only title+content_type, never copied content); a missing block shows '(stale reference)', never a copy (AC1/AC4). NO PERPETUAL SPINNER - loading animates ONLY during a genuine in-flight fetch (runtime present); headless neutral (MT-015 lesson); kittest uses step() not run(). Viewport persistence fires on pan/zoom RELEASE or a 300ms debounce - NEVER every frame (RISK-3/MC-3). OS external drag/drop is LIMITED in egui - implement inter-panel drag via egui::DragAndDrop (MIME application/x-handshake-loom-block); external OS drop is BEST-EFFORT with a documented paste/text-field FALLBACK (RISK-2/MC-2), never a faked drop. Status bar confirms 'Removed placement (source block kept)' (RISK-4/MC-4). All HTTP via backend_client.rs (no Tauri). HBR-QUIET: drag/edge/zoom never steal OS focus. Screenshots to the EXTERNAL wp-kernel-012-mt-026 root + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: Group/Status/Button - field-correct accesskit 0.21.1; ACs assert author_ids.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.tsx — entire component (primary reference). All behaviors listed in the step-by-step notes above map 1:1 to this file.
Canvas board API types: app/src/lib/api.ts lines 1617-1750 (approximate)

risks: RISK-1: Canvas coordinates drift if pan and zoom are applied inconsistently between drawing pass and hit-test pass. Mitigation: define a single canvas_to_screen(pos) and screen_to_canvas(pos) function pair and use them exclusively; unit test the round-trip.

RISK-2: egui's DragAndDrop API may not support external OS drops or inter-panel drags in all backends. Mitigation: implement a fallback where a 'Drag block here' instruction panel is shown and the user can also paste a block_id via a text field.

RISK-3: Viewport persistence (updateCanvasBoardViewport) fires too frequently during smooth pan/zoom, causing excessive API load. Mitigation: debounce viewport persistence 300ms after last pan/zoom event; do not fire on every frame.
RISK-4: removeCanvasPlacement might be confused with block deletion by the user. Mitigation: status bar must confirm 'Removed placement {id} (source block kept)' exactly as in the React reference.
RISK-5: Dashed-line manual implementation may produce incorrect segment counts for very short edges (< one dash). Mitigation: if line length < 10px, draw as a solid short line.

RISK-6: Simultaneous semantic edge creation and visual edge creation with the same selection could double-mutate. Mitigation: edge_from is cleared immediately on second card click before any async call; the async call is fire-and-forget with error surface only.
minimum_controls: MC-1: canvas_to_screen / screen_to_canvas pair tested round-trip. Covered by PROOF1.
MC-2: Fallback text-field block placement in case OS drag is unavailable. Covered by AC4 (drop test) + implementation notes.
MC-3: 300ms debounce on viewport persistence. Covered by AC2 (viewport persisted).
MC-4: Status bar confirms placement removal not block deletion. Covered by AC8 and PROOF5.
MC-5: Short edge solid line fallback. Covered by PROOF6 (visual smoke test).

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-022/023/024 backend-shape lesson + MT-015 idle-repaint + WP-011 canvas_board reuse
corrections: graph/canvas_board.rs (distinct from the root WP-011 canvas_board.rs to reuse); backend_client+test in allowed_paths
VERIFY content_type=canvas + the full canvas API + placement shape vs real backend (don't assume)
real-PG canvas data NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; transform/hit-test/dashed-edge/empty standalone
reference-not-copy live title once-on-load; no perpetual spinner; viewport-persist on release not per-frame; OS-drop best-effort+fallback; external screenshots; accesskit variant

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_e0569070 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: 9e338cd4 feat MT-026 native LoomCanvasBoard (E3 Obsidian-Canvas parity)
implementation_result.review_panel: correctness=CHANGES_REQUIRED(1), backend-shape=PASS(0) (impl pre-verified+corrected routes), perf-focus-hygiene=PASS(0) -> 1 must-fix hardened.
implementation_result.proofs: AC2/3/5/6/7/9/10 + PROOF1/2/4/5/6 PASS; AC1/AC4/AC8(source-kept) live-PG = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (gated); 934 lib + 10 unit + 16 integration green; canvas board (Pan/zoom 1.00x + Text card/Group/Edge:Semantic toolbar + dotted grid + 2 reference cards Block 1/Block 2 + remove + '2 placements') visually verified; transform round-trip <1px (one canvas_to_screen/screen_to_canvas pair); reference-not-copy live titles; screenshot EXTERNAL (git ls-files src/**/*.png empty).
implementation_result.backend_shape_corrected: Routes verified + corrected vs the real backend: PATCH /loom/canvas-placements/:id (group_id), DELETE /loom/canvas-placements/:id, POST /loom/edges {edge_type:'mention',created_by:'user'}, POST .../visual-edges {from/to_placement_id}, POST .../placements + /cards + PUT /viewport. content_type=canvas verified.
implementation_result.corrections_held: graph/canvas_board.rs (distinct from the root WP-011 canvas_board.rs whose pan/zoom it reuses); reference-not-copy (live title once-on-load, '(stale reference)' for missing); viewport-persist on release; OS-drop best-effort via egui::DragAndDrop + paste-block-id fallback (RISK-2); status 'Removed placement (source block kept)'; backend_client (no Tauri); no perpetual spinner.
